frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: decisions formed by integrating ambiguous evidence over time

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: choice bias decomposed from sensitivity in detection tasks

frameworks.name: pupil-linked arousal
frameworks.one_line: phasic arousal indexed by evoked pupil dilation modulates bias

core_question: Does pupil-linked phasic arousal suppress choice biases during evidence-accumulation decisions across species and decision domains?
key_assumptions: evoked pupil dilation indexes phasic arousal-system activation
choice bias can be separated from sensitivity within an accumulation framework
perceptual and memory-based decisions share common accumulation machinery
manipulations: phasic arousal level (high vs low pupil response trials)
bias direction (spontaneous conservative vs liberal; experimentally induced)
key_findings: high phasic arousal reduces impact of choice biases in sound-detection tasks
bias suppression acts on both conservative and liberal biases
effect generalizes from humans to mice
effect generalizes from perceptual to memory-based decisions
interpretations: phasic arousal selectively reduces biases in evidence accumulation rather than sensitivity
interplay between phasic arousal and decision-making reflects a general cross-species principle
